EX 80 – VNA Replenishment Task Combining McLane has Very Narrow Aisles (VNAs) which are a lso referred to as Turret Aisles, in which McLane is wanting the p ic king operator to only do one pass through the aisl e and pick all the replenishment demand for an item at one time . In order to fulfill this , WM is modified to combine less than full pallet allocations for a certain item/iLPN /destination together in an attempt to reduce the amount of tasks needed for certain items . Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y Allocation records combination WM invokes t he following logic during the wave , right after allocations have been created and before the task creation has been initiated . Once the wave has created allocations WM assesses the less than pallet replenishment allocations which fall under the certain Start WG/WA and Destination WG/WA combinations specified in the E80 system code. Allocations which share the same item, source iLPN and target location are combined into one allocation record . The original allocation records are canceled . Only the allocations within the wave are assessed. Assumptions The VNA locations have different WG/WA than non-VNA locations including the drop locations Only Reserve locations are found in the VNA WG/WA No mixed pallets are putaway to VNA locations The re is no Returns receiving to VNA locations The VNA locations are setup to have a Max User Per Aisle = 1 The Turret Operators are in a different task group than the dock haulers This modific ation only affects INT1 and INT9 replenishments as it is assumed that the VNA locations are Reserve locations This modification does not apply to INT3 replenishments This modification does not apply to Cigarette items (assumed that the start WG/WA and destination WG/WA combination for cigarettes replenishments will not be configured in E80 Code Id) Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Allocation Inventory Detail record updates Enablement IF C-00 0 -000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF C-000-EX 80 byte 1 = Y (EX 80 extension enablement) ELSE the EX 80 functionality is disabled ELSE the EX 80 functionality is disabled ELSE the EX 80 functionality disabled Validations WM firs t validates that there is at least one code id in the s ystem code C-E80 , if no code id’s exist then WM exists out of the custom logic. Updates Once the above validations have passed WM carries out the below logic in order to create new allocations by combining partial allocations together. During a wave and after allocation creation but before task creation WM searches for any INT1 or INT9 less than full pallet allocation records and takes note of the iLPNs WM then takes these iLPNs and searches for any INT1 or INT9 allocations which have the iLPN and whos Pull and Destination location combination that match one of the Start WG/WA and Destination WG/WA combinations specified by the C-E80 system code Code IDs. Then WM assess all these allocation records to see if there are any records which share the same item, source iLPN, and destination location. If there are any allocation records that ma tch then If th e allocation are all from the current wave then Create a new allocation record for the total combined quantity, be sure to keep the link to any INT50/51 allocation records of the original INT1/9 allocation records WM takes the priority from the allocation with the highest priority (lowest priority number) If the total qty is LESS than the total iLPN q uantity, then leave the allocation UOM alone. If the total qty is EQUAL to the total iLPN q uantity, then change alloc ation UOM to C . Delete the original allocation records Technical notes The below query can be used to find allocations that can be combined . Select AID.* from alloc_invn_dtl AID Where AID.INVN_NEED_TYPE in ('9','1') and AID.ITEM_ID = ' item id’ and AID.CNTR_NBR = ' source iLPN ' and AID.DEST_LOCN_ID in (select LOCN_ID from LOCN_HDR where Work_grp ='work group' and Work_area ='work area') Navigation No changes to base navigation as part of this extension Outputs No changes to outputs as part of this extension. Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base LM Hooks as part of this extension . Process Change/Addition No additional process changes in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
